channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Week 16 game between Denver Broncos and Los Angeles Chargers flexed to Thursday night spot
item.description: The Los Angeles Chargers have played their way into another prime time appearance. Justin Herbert and company have had their Week 16 home game against the Denver Broncos flexed from Sunday, Dec. 22, to Thursday night, Dec. 19.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: F1 closer to approving expanded grid for General Motors entry initially started by Michael Andretti
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — With a restructuring at Andretti Global that pushed Michael Andretti into a smaller role, the chances of his organization landing a Formula 1 team have substantially increased.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: St. Petersburg council rejects immediate repair to Rays' ballpark roof after first giving approval
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The St. Petersburg City Council reversed course Thursday on whether to spend more than $23 million to repair the hurricane-shredded roof of the Tampa Bay Rays' ballpark, initially voting narrowly for approval and hours later changing course.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Martin Slumbers hangs his hat on making British Open big and promoting women's golf
item.description: Martin Slumbers could not have witnessed greater theater than his first British Open in charge of the R&A, the record-setting duel between Henrik Stenson and Phil Mickelson at Royal Troon. His only concern in 2016 was the audience.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bad timing for 49ers? Brock Purdy is hurting while Jordan Love is getting healthy
item.description: 49ers quarterback Brock Purdy, rush end Nick Bosa and tackle Trent Williams are all hurting. In Green Bay, quarterback Jordan Love is finally healthy.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Rafael Nadal retired after the Davis Cup. It's a rare team event in tennis
item.description: Rafael Nadal wanted to play his last match before retiring at home in Spain and as part of Spain's Davis Cup team.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fendrich, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Browns use blizzard conditions to upset AFC North rival Steelers in 4th quarter comeback
item.description: The Cleveland Browns, with blizzard conditions swirling all around their home stadium, upset the Pittsburgh Steelers behind Nick Chubb's two-touchdown night, including the game-winner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: If the Jets don't want Aaron Rodgers back, the 4-time MVP will find another home: Analysis
item.description: On Football analyzes the biggest topics in the NFL from week to week. For more On Football analysis, head here. ___ Joe Douglas is gone. Robert Saleh already was fired. Aaron Rodgers could be next to leave the New York Jets.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here's Jason: Kelce will host late-night show on ESPN beginning final week of NFL season
item.description: Jason Kelce will try his hand at late-night television early next year. Kelce announced during an appearance on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live!” on Thursday night that he will host “They Call It Late Night with Jason Kelce,” on ESPN.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crimson Cliffs pulls off miraculous comeback to win 4A title
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was simply one of the best, most dramatic games in Utah high school football championship game history.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: CMU women's soccer "feeling pretty good" ahead of NCAA tournament
item.description: Colorado Mesa women’s soccer is in. The Mavs received an at-large bid into the Division II NCAA tournament.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Zach Werenski’s goal in OT and 5-point game helps Blue Jackets to 7-6 win over Lightning
item.description: Zach Werenski’s goal at 1:25 of overtime punctuated a five-point game as the Columbus Blue Jackets rallied from an early three-goal deficit and beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 7-6. Werenski had tied the game at 4 in the second period and added three assists.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Biden welcomes 2024 NBA champion Boston Celtics to White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House was tinted green on Thursday, when President Joe Biden — Secret Service code name: Celtic — welcomed “Coach Joe” Mazzulla and the Boston Celtics to the South Lawn to celebrate the franchise’s unprecedented 18th NBA championship.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rays new stadium deal in play: Pinellas commissioner, St. Pete mayor
item.description: St. Petersburg resident and former Raymond James Executive Ron Diner is with the group No Home Run. They've been against a new Tampa Bay Rays stadium deal from the start.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MLB will test robot umpires at 13 spring training ballparks hosting 19 teams
item.description: MLB has been experimenting with the automated ball-strike system in the minor leagues since 2019 but is still working on the shape of the strike zone.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Evans trending towards playing against the Giants on Sunday
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are looking to get back on track after four straight losses against the Giants, who swapped starting quarterbacks after losing five in a row.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alex Killorn helps the Ducks beat the Blackhawks 3-2 for their third straight win
item.description: Alex Killorn had a goal and an assist in the third period, and the Anaheim Ducks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Tuesday night for their season-high third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Jets are a dumpster fire
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The Jets are a remarkable dumpster fire. Head coach Robert Saleh fired. GM Joe Douglas fired. Offensive coordinator Nathaniel Hackett demoted. They are a 3 and 8 football team and will most certainly miss the playoffs for the 14th consecutive year.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Lynne Roberts leaves Utah for WNBA job
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In a shocking announcement Tuesday evening, Lynne Roberts is leaving the University of Utah women's basketball program to become the head coach of the Los Angeles Sparks of the WNBA.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz comeback falls short against Clippers, 116-105
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - James Harden moved into second place on the NBA's career 3-point list and had 20 points and 11 assists to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Utah Jazz 116-105 on Sunday night to end a three-game losing streak. Harden moved past Ray Allen with No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sengun records triple-double, VanVleet scores 28 in Rockets 143-107 rout of Bulls
item.description: Fred VanVleet scored a season-high 28 points, Alperen Sengun had 20 points, 11 rebounds and 11 assists for his second triple-double this season and the Houston Rockets routed the Chicago Bulls 143-107 on Sunday night for their fifth straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jake Paul, 27, beats 58-year-old Mike Tyson as the hits don't match the hype
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — The boos from a crowd wanting more action were growing again when Jake Paul dropped his gloves before the final bell and bowed toward 58-year-old Mike Tyson.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US skier Mikaela Shiffrin wins 1st World Cup slalom of season for her 98th career victory
item.description: American ski star Mikaela Shiffrin earned her record-extending 98th career World Cup win Saturday to give herself the chance to compete for victory No. 100 in front of a home crowd.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
